purpose: Stages fiscal dominance progression from early warning to entrenched regime, providing an organizing heuristic for where a country stands on the spectrum.
components: Signal 1: Persistent primary deficits above structural threshold
Signal 2: r-g convergence and debt dynamics deterioration
Signal 4: Term premium reflecting fiscal supply rather than monetary policy
Signal 5: Inflation expectations de-anchoring and formal financial repression
current_assessment: US scores 2.0-2.5 of 5 signals as of early 2026, with Japan circa 2005-10 as closest analogue. Currently on the gradual path (<50bp/quarter term premium velocity).
limitations: Fitted to n=3-4 cases with zero degrees of freedom — definitionally over-fitted
Not falsifiable: no specified time horizon, no progression probability, no disconfirmation criteria
Signal 3 is inherently ambiguous and not operationally measurable
'2.5 of 5' should NOT be read as '50% of the way to fiscal dominance'
Appropriate for scenario organization, not probability estimation
